Steps.Name: Open Process
Steps.Description: Obtains a handle to the target process. Required before any cross-process memory or thread operations can be performed.

Parameters.Name: dwDesiredAccess
Parameters.Description: Requested access rights for the target process handle, determining what operations the caller can perform — such as reading/writing process memory, creating threads, or terminating the process.

Parameters.Name: bInheritHandle
Parameters.Description: Whether child processes spawned by the caller can inherit this handle. Set to FALSE — no handle inheritance is needed for injection.

Parameters.Name: dwProcessId
Parameters.Description: The PID of the target process to open. Supplied externally by the caller as the injection target.

Steps.Name: Virtual Allocation
Steps.Description: Allocates a region of memory inside the target process's address space where the payload will be written and later executed.

Parameters.Name: hProcess
Parameters.Description: Handle to the target process obtained from OpenProcess. Determines where the allocation occurs.

Parameters.Name: lpAddress
Parameters.Description: Desired starting address for the allocation. NULL lets the OS choose a suitable address.

Parameters.Name: dwSize
Parameters.Description: Size in bytes of the region to allocate. Matches the payload length to fit it exactly.

Parameters.Name: flAllocationType
Parameters.Description: MEM_RESERVE reserves the address range; MEM_COMMIT backs it with physical memory. Both flags together make the region immediately usable.

Parameters.Name: flProtect
Parameters.Description: Memory protection for the allocated region. PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE allows the payload to be written and then executed directly — a strong indicator of code injection.

Steps.Name: Write Process Memory
Steps.Description: Copies the shellcode payload from the caller's address space into the allocated region inside the target process.

Parameters.Name: hProcess
Parameters.Description: Handle to the target process. Determines which process's memory is written to.

Parameters.Name: lpBaseAddress
Parameters.Description: Destination address inside the target process — the buffer returned by VirtualAllocEx.

Parameters.Name: lpBuffer
Parameters.Description: Pointer to the source payload in the caller's memory that will be copied into the target.

Parameters.Name: nSize
Parameters.Description: Number of bytes to write. Matches the full payload length.

Parameters.Name: lpNumberOfBytesWritten
Parameters.Description: Optional output for how many bytes were actually written. NULL means the caller is not checking this.

Steps.Name: Create Remote Thread
Steps.Description: Spawns a new thread inside the target process with its entry point set to the injected payload, triggering execution of the shellcode.

Parameters.Name: hProcess
Parameters.Description: Handle to the target process where the new thread will be created.

Parameters.Name: lpThreadAttributes
Parameters.Description: Security attributes for the thread handle. NULL uses default security — no special inheritance or descriptor.

Parameters.Name: dwStackSize
Parameters.Description: Initial stack size for the new thread. 0 uses the executable's default stack size.

Parameters.Name: lpStartAddress
Parameters.Description: Entry point of the new thread — cast to the allocated remote buffer, so the thread begins executing the injected payload.

Parameters.Name: lpParameter
Parameters.Description: Argument passed to the thread function. NULL — no parameter is needed for this payload.

Parameters.Name: dwCreationFlags
Parameters.Description: Thread creation flags. 0 means the thread starts running immediately after creation.

Parameters.Name: lpThreadId
Parameters.Description: Optional output for the new thread's ID. NULL — the caller does not need to track the thread ID.

Steps.Name: Create Suspended Process
Steps.Description: Spawns the target executable in a suspended state before its entry point runs. This gives the attacker a window to inject code before any user-mode code in the target has executed.

Parameters.Name: lpApplicationName
Parameters.Description: Path to the target executable to spawn. The attacker controls which process becomes the injection host.

Parameters.Name: dwCreationFlags
Parameters.Description: CREATE_SUSPENDED halts the new process at its entry point before any user-mode code runs — the critical flag that enables Early Bird injection.

Parameters.Name: lpProcessInformation
Parameters.Description: Receives the handles to the new process (hProcess) and its main thread (hThread), both needed for subsequent injection steps.

Steps.Name: Virtual Allocation
Steps.Description: Allocates a RWX memory region inside the suspended process where the payload will be written.

Parameters.Name: hProcess
Parameters.Description: Handle to the suspended target process obtained from CreateProcessA.

Parameters.Name: flAllocationType
Parameters.Description: MEM_COMMIT | MEM_RESERVE makes the region immediately usable without a separate reservation step.

Parameters.Name: flProtect
Parameters.Description: PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE allows the region to be written now and executed later when the APC fires — a strong behavioral indicator.

Steps.Name: Write Process Memory
Steps.Description: Copies the shellcode into the allocated region of the suspended process.

Parameters.Name: hProcess
Parameters.Description: Handle to the suspended target process.

Parameters.Name: lpBaseAddress
Parameters.Description: Destination address in the target — the region returned by VirtualAllocEx.

Parameters.Name: lpNumberOfBytesWritten
Parameters.Description: Output checked against the payload length. A mismatch triggers cleanup and abort — unlike Simple Injection which ignores this.

Steps.Name: Queue User APC
Steps.Description: Queues an Asynchronous Procedure Call to the suspended main thread pointing at the injected payload. When the thread is resumed it enters an alertable state and drains the APC queue, executing the shellcode before the process's own entry point.

Parameters.Name: pfnAPC
Parameters.Description: The APC callback — cast to PAPCFUNC and pointed at blockMem, so the shellcode is what executes when the APC fires.

Parameters.Name: hThread
Parameters.Description: The main thread of the suspended process. Queuing the APC here guarantees it runs before any application code.

Parameters.Name: dwData
Parameters.Description: Optional parameter passed to the APC function. 0 — the shellcode does not expect an argument.

Steps.Name: Resume Thread
Steps.Description: Lifts the suspension on the main thread. As the thread initializes it enters an alertable wait, draining the APC queue and executing the shellcode before the process's own entry point code ever runs.

Parameters.Name: hThread
Parameters.Description: The suspended main thread of the target process. Resuming it triggers APC execution.

Steps.Name: Lookup Privilege Value
Steps.Description: Resolves the locally unique identifier (LUID) for SeDebugPrivilege. This LUID is required before the privilege can be enabled on the current process token.

Parameters.Name: lpSystemName
Parameters.Description: NULL means the lookup is performed on the local system.

Parameters.Name: lpName
Parameters.Description: SE_DEBUG_NAME — the privilege that allows opening handles to processes owned by other users, including SYSTEM.

Parameters.Name: lpLuid
Parameters.Description: Output — receives the LUID that identifies SeDebugPrivilege on this system.

Steps.Name: Open Current Process Token
Steps.Description: Opens the access token of the current process so that SeDebugPrivilege can be enabled on it.

Parameters.Name: ProcessHandle
Parameters.Description: GetCurrentProcess() — a pseudo-handle to the calling process.

Parameters.Name: DesiredAccess
Parameters.Description: TOKEN_ADJUST_PRIVILEGES — the minimum access right needed to enable or disable privileges on the token.

Parameters.Name: TokenHandle
Parameters.Description: Output — receives the handle to the current process token.

Steps.Name: Adjust Token Privileges
Steps.Description: Enables SeDebugPrivilege on the current process token. Without this, OpenProcess on a privileged target process would fail.

Parameters.Name: TokenHandle
Parameters.Description: The current process token opened in the previous step.

Parameters.Name: DisableAllPrivileges
Parameters.Description: FALSE — only modify the specific privileges listed in NewState, not disable all.

Parameters.Name: NewState
Parameters.Description: TOKEN_PRIVILEGES struct with SE_PRIVILEGE_ENABLED set on the SeDebugPrivilege LUID.

Steps.Name: Open Target Process
Steps.Description: Opens a handle to the target process using PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED_INFORMATION — the minimum right needed to read its token. SeDebugPrivilege makes this succeed even on privileged processes.

Parameters.Name: dwDesiredAccess
Parameters.Description: PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED_INFORMATION — only needs to query the process token, not full access.

Parameters.Name: dwProcessId
Parameters.Description: PID of the privileged target process whose token will be stolen.

Steps.Name: Open Target Process Token
Steps.Description: Retrieves the access token of the target privileged process. This token will be duplicated and used to spawn a new process under that identity.

Parameters.Name: ProcessHandle
Parameters.Description: Handle to the target process obtained from OpenProcess.

Parameters.Name: DesiredAccess
Parameters.Description: MAXIMUM_ALLOWED — requests the broadest access the caller is permitted, ensuring the token can later be duplicated.

Parameters.Name: TokenHandle
Parameters.Description: Output — receives the handle to the target process token.

Steps.Name: Duplicate Token
Steps.Description: Creates a copy of the target's token as a primary token. A primary token is required for CreateProcessWithTokenW — impersonation tokens alone cannot be used to spawn a process.

Parameters.Name: hExistingToken
Parameters.Description: The token obtained from the target process.

Parameters.Name: ImpersonationLevel
Parameters.Description: SecurityImpersonation — allows the token to impersonate the target identity on the local system.

Parameters.Name: TokenType
Parameters.Description: TokenPrimary — produces a token suitable for creating a new process, not just impersonation.

Parameters.Name: phNewToken
Parameters.Description: Output — receives the duplicated primary token that will be passed to CreateProcessWithTokenW.

Steps.Name: Create Process With Token
Steps.Description: Spawns cmd.exe under the identity of the stolen token, effectively running as the target privileged user.

Parameters.Name: hToken
Parameters.Description: The duplicated primary token from the target process. This determines the security context of the spawned process.

Parameters.Name: dwLogonFlags
Parameters.Description: LOGON_WITH_PROFILE — loads the user profile of the token owner, making the environment match the target identity.

Parameters.Name: lpApplicationName
Parameters.Description: Full path to cmd.exe — the process spawned under the stolen identity.

Steps.Name: Create Registry Key
Steps.Description: Creates or opens the ms-settings shell command registry key under HKCU. This key is checked by fodhelper.exe on launch — writing here is the core of the UAC bypass since HKCU requires no elevation to write.

Parameters.Name: hKey
Parameters.Description: HKEY_CURRENT_USER — writable by any user without elevation, making this bypass work from a medium-integrity process.

Parameters.Name: lpSubKey
Parameters.Description: The ms-settings shell command path that fodhelper.exe queries when it auto-elevates.

Parameters.Name: samDesired
Parameters.Description: KEY_WRITE — only write access is needed to plant the payload values.

Steps.Name: Set Command Value
Steps.Description: Writes the command to execute into the default value of the registry key. When fodhelper.exe reads this key and auto-elevates, it will run this command with high integrity.

Parameters.Name: lpValueName
Parameters.Description: NULL — sets the default (unnamed) value of the key, which is what fodhelper reads as the command to run.

Parameters.Name: dwType
Parameters.Description: REG_SZ — a null-terminated string value.

Parameters.Name: lpData
Parameters.Description: The command to execute with elevated privileges — in this case launching cmd.exe.

Steps.Name: Set DelegateExecute Value
Steps.Description: Creates the DelegateExecute value in the same key. Its mere presence — even as an empty string — signals to the Windows shell that the command should be auto-elevated, completing the hijack condition.

Parameters.Name: lpValueName
Parameters.Description: DelegateExecute — the specific value name the shell checks to decide whether to auto-elevate the command.

Parameters.Name: lpData
Parameters.Description: Empty string — the value does not need content. Its existence is sufficient to trigger auto-elevation.

Steps.Name: Execute FodHelper
Steps.Description: Launches fodhelper.exe with the runas verb. Because fodhelper.exe is marked as auto-elevate in its manifest, Windows elevates it without showing a UAC prompt to the user. The elevated process then reads the hijacked registry key and executes the planted command.

Parameters.Name: lpVerb
Parameters.Description: runas — requests elevated execution. Combined with fodhelper's auto-elevate manifest flag, this bypasses the UAC consent dialog entirely.

Parameters.Name: lpFile
Parameters.Description: fodhelper.exe — a trusted Windows binary with the auto-elevate manifest flag set, making it the bypass vehicle.

Parameters.Name: nShow
Parameters.Description: SW_NORMAL — shows the spawned window normally.
